name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Aviation Sector
owner: Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism, Japan (MLIT), Civil Aviation Bureau
description: 7th edition, revised April 30, 2026 (152 pages). Covers cybersecurity requirements for airline operational systems (flight ops, reservation systems) as designated critical infrastructure. Includes a full revision history back to the 1st edition.
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/mlit.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain.

current_extraction.notes: Full text acquired cleanly. An earlier session pass had characterized MLIT's guidelines as "image/scan-heavy, resisting text extraction" based on WebFetch's built-in PDF handling -- that assessment was wrong. Plain pdftotext -layout extracted ~450,000 characters of clean, readable Japanese text with no OCR needed.
